children.heading: Definition
children.label: a Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “unmanned aircraft” has the meaning given the term in .section 44801 of title 49

children.heading: Increased penalties for operation of unmanned aircraft to facilitate felony offense
children.label: c Increased penalties for operation of unmanned aircraft to facilitate felony offense
children.text: If a person who is convicted of a felony offense (other than an offense based solely on the operation of an unmanned aircraft) knowingly operated an unmanned aircraft during, in relation to, or in furtherance of such offense, the maximum imprisonment otherwise provided by law for that offense shall be doubled or increased by 5 years, whichever is less.

children.heading: Increased penalties for use of unmanned aircraft to introduce contraband into prisons
children.label: d Increased penalties for use of unmanned aircraft to introduce contraband into prisons
children.text: If a defendant who is convicted under knowingly used an unmanned aircraft to provide a prohibited object to an inmate of a prison, the maximum imprisonment otherwise provided by law for that offense shall be increased by 5 years.section 1791 of title 18

children.heading: Directive to United States Sentencing Commission: enhanced sentencing range for use of unmanned aircraft
children.label: e Directive to United States Sentencing Commission: enhanced sentencing range for use of unmanned aircraft
A
children.text: promulgate guidelines, or amendments to guidelines, that substantially increase the sentencing range for all offenses involving the use of an unmanned aircraft; and

children.label: B
children.text: as necessary, promulgate policy statements, or amendments to policy statements to assist in the application of this section.

children.heading: Enhanced penalties
children.label: 2 Enhanced penalties
children.text: In any case in which the enhanced penalties of subsection (c) apply, the guidelines and amendments issued under paragraph (1) shall call for an increase of at least 6 levels in the base offense level and in all other cases, the base offense level shall be increased by at least 4 levels.

children.heading: Penalties for unauthorized counter-UAS actions
children.label: f Penalties for unauthorized counter-UAS actions

children.text: a civil fine up to $100,000 per violation; or
suspension of counter-UAS authority pending review by the Attorney General or Secretary of Homeland Security.

children.heading: Civil enforcement
children.label: g Civil enforcement
children.text: The Attorney General is authorized to bring a civil action in a United States district court to collect fines and enforce civil penalties imposed under this section.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: h Effective date
children.text: This section and the amendments made by this section shall take effect 30 days after .December 18, 2025
